paragraph: North Arlington's single-family market is straightforward and competitive right now. Thirty-one homes sold over the last seven months at a median of $630,000, with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.03 and roughly 68 percent of homes selling above asking. Median days on market sits at 20, which means priced homes are moving without excessive wait time. The spread runs from $460,000 to $1,155,000, so you'll find entry points at the lower end and some higher-end properties that command real money. Multi-family properties, a smaller segment with 10 sales, show median pricing of $842,500 and a 1.0 sale-to-list ratio, which indicates buyers are holding the line on price. The multi-family market isn't as heated as single-family, but homes are still moving in under 16 days on average. If you're looking in North Arlington, the market is competitive but not frenzied. Sellers who price realistically will move their homes, and buyers with flexibility on timing or property type will find options at multiple price points.
